title: Data Scan
heading: Data Scan Rules
description: Select a category below to view its specific data scan checks.

categories.title: Global Variable Checks
items: Contains all expected categories.
If ask all has base of total n.
If filtered (question or iterations) has filtered/reduced base.
All categories within a variable have data or an expected zero.

categories.title: COMP, Terminations & Sampling
items: COMP: Sense check of statuses based on project expectations.
COMP: Should not have Script Errors or other unusual statuses.
COMP: Should not have unexpected OQs.
COMP: Should not have a majority of drop-offs/breakoffs.
DataCollection.EndQuestion: Should primarily be early questions/intro or the final question.
Verify functionality of all terms check logically if possible, especially for untriggered terms.
Link Type: Verify functionality of Link Type logic for special links and augments.

categories.title: Quota Tables
items: Quota tables do not contain any -1 or 0-normal target values.
Quota tables for assigners contain appropriate targets based on random/most need methodology.
Quota tables for most needed assigners are not set to counter.
Quotas that are filtered to only a subset of respondents are filtering correctly.
For international/market-based studies, quota tables are split by market and/or link type.

categories.title: Assigners
items: Eligibility variables fully populated for eligible respondents.
Most Need assignment: Items should be assigned relatively evenly based on targets.
Random assignment: Items should be assigned proportional to eligibility.
Priority Assignments: Priority items should be assigned whenever eligible.
Respondents not eligible for an assigner: handled as directed (term, skipping, etc.).

categories.title: Specialized Question Checks
items: QC Flags: Confirm logic and no unusual incidence.
Segmentation algorithms: Classifying all applicable respondents.
DCM/MaxDiff: Version Assignment handled via mostneed assigner.
PBS exercises: data flowing correctly for appropriate filtered or assigned brands.
Open-Ends: any obvious response quality issues?
Property-based filtering: ensuring correct application.
